checks.brianTurnedAway: Brian's name doesn't start with "Brad" — he should be turned away, but he wasn't.

checks.silenceTurnedAway: The person didn't say their name — they should be turned away, but they weren't.

checkTheName.name: Check the name
checkTheName.description: Ask the person their name and check if it starts with the allowed letters for tonight's party. If it does, let them in. Otherwise, turn them away.

sarahSParty.name: S Party: Sarah arrives
sarahSParty.description: Tonight only names starting with "S" are allowed. Sarah should get in!

bradSParty.name: S Party: Brad arrives
bradSParty.description: Tonight only names starting with "S" are allowed. Brad should be turned away.

bradleyBradParty.name: Brad Party: Bradley arrives
bradleyBradParty.description: Tonight only names starting with "Brad" are allowed. Bradley should get in!

brianBradParty.name: Brad Party: Brian arrives
brianBradParty.description: Tonight only names starting with "Brad" are allowed. Brian should be turned away.

silence.name: S Party: Silence...
silence.description: The person doesn't say their name. An empty name can't start with anything — turn them away!

cherCherParty.name: Cher Party: Cher arrives
cherCherParty.description: Tonight only names starting with "Cher" are allowed. Cher's name is exactly "Cher" — let her in!

getNameAndStart.question: How do I get the name and the allowed start?
getNameAndStart.answer: Call `askName()` and `getAllowedStart()` and store the results in variables so you can use them in your comparison.

checkStartsWith.question: How do I check that a name starts with certain letters?
checkStartsWith.answer: Compare each character of the allowed start with the character at the same position in the name. If any character differs, the name doesn't match.

loopCharacters.question: How do I look at one character at a time?
loopCharacters.answer: Use a counter variable starting at `1` and step through each position with a loop, comparing `name` and `allowedStart` at that position.

stopComparing.question: How do I know when to stop comparing?
stopComparing.answer: Stop when you've checked every character of the allowed start. You'll need its length for that, similar to what you did in Sign Painter Price.

longerAllowedStart.question: What if the allowed start is longer than the name?
longerAllowedStart.answer: That person can't match, so they should be turned away. Check the lengths before you start comparing and handle that case.

askName.description: Returns the **name** of the person at the door.

getAllowedStart.description: Returns the required **starting letters** for tonight's party.

letIn.description: Lets the person into the party.

turnAway.description: Turns the person away from the party.
